setup: m.blk[:1], real q/k/v producer (proj+q/k-norm+rope), opaque append into fp16 cache, owned tile read, real prefill, eager multi-step

findings.cache_after_append: NaN (the append writes NaN to cache[start_pos] given FINITE input k)
findings.cache_fill_method: NaN under BOTH canonical-store prefill AND assign-fill -> NOT the cache fill
findings.src_realize_fix: BLOCKED -- '.realize()' inside the model forward raises 'usage of device AMD disallowed' (ALLOW_DEVICE_USAGE)

root_cause: The model decode runs under CALLIFY (pure-function compilation; tinygrad/device.py:25 ALLOW_DEVICE_USAGE guard). Callify disallows the eager mutable-buffer realize that the opaque append requires -> inside the callified forward the append's src cannot be realized in order, so the append reads unrealized data and writes NaN. The TOY passes ONLY because it is plain TinyJit (NOT callified). This is NOT the append kernel (toy passes), NOT persistence/args/dtype/cache/prefill (all refuted) -- it is the pure-function EXECUTION MODEL.
